### FILE: scripts/upgrade_to_admin.sql (20 lines)

```sql
-- Upgrade an account to admin role
-- Replace 'your-email@example.com' with the email of the account you want to upgrade

UPDATE user_profiles
SET
  role = 'admin',
  spa_name = 'Platform Administrator'
WHERE id = (
  SELECT id FROM auth.users
  WHERE email = 'your-email@example.com'
);

-- Verify the upgrade
SELECT
  au.email,
  up.role,
  up.spa_name
FROM user_profiles up
JOIN auth.users au ON up.id = au.id
WHERE au.email = 'your-email@example.com';

```

---

## SECTION 3: ROUTE MAP

Complete application route structure with authentication requirements:

```
PUBLIC ROUTES (No Auth Required)
├─ / → PublicHome → No → None
├─ /brands → PublicBrands → No → None

BUSINESS PORTAL ROUTES
├─ /portal → BusinessLayout → No → None
├─ /portal/login → BusinessLogin → No → None
├─ /portal/signup → BusinessSignup → No → None
├─ /portal/dashboard → BusinessDashboard → Yes → Any authenticated
├─ /portal/plans → PlansList → Yes → Any authenticated
├─ /portal/plans/new → PlanWizard → Yes → Any authenticated
├─ /portal/plans/:id → PlanResults → Yes → Any authenticated
├─ /portal/brands/:slug → BrandDetail → No → None

BRAND PORTAL ROUTES
├─ /brand/login → BrandLogin → No → None
├─ /brand → BrandLayout → Yes → brand_admin, admin, platform_admin
├─ /brand/dashboard → BrandDashboard → Yes → brand_admin, admin, platform_admin
├─ /brand/plans → BrandPlans → Yes → brand_admin, admin, platform_admin
├─ /brand/leads → BrandLeads → Yes → brand_admin, admin, platform_admin

SPA PORTAL ROUTES (Legacy)
├─ /spa/login → SpaLogin → No → None
├─ /spa/signup → Navigate to /portal/signup (redirect)
├─ /spa → SpaLayout → Yes → spa_user
├─ /spa/dashboard → SpaDashboard → Yes → spa_user
├─ /spa/plans → SpaPlans → Yes → spa_user
├─ /spa/plans/new → SpaPlanWizard → Yes → spa_user
├─ /spa/plans/:id → SpaPlanDetail → Yes → spa_user
├─ /spa/products → SpaProductLibrary → Yes → spa_user
├─ /spa/services → SpaServiceLibrary → Yes → spa_user

ADMIN PORTAL ROUTES
├─ /admin/login → AdminLogin → No → None
├─ /admin → AdminLayout → Yes → admin, platform_admin
├─ /admin/brands → BrandsAdminList → Yes → admin, platform_admin
├─ /admin/brands/:id → BrandAdminEditor → Yes → admin, platform_admin
├─ /admin/inbox → AdminInbox → Yes → admin, platform_admin
├─ /admin/debug → AdminDebugPanel → Yes → admin, platform_admin
├─ /admin/submissions/:id → Placeholder (coming soon) → Yes → admin, platform_admin
├─ /admin/ingestion → IngestionView → Yes → admin, platform_admin
├─ /admin/protocols → ProtocolsView → Yes → admin, platform_admin
├─ /admin/products → Products Grid (PRO/Retail) → Yes → admin, platform_admin
├─ /admin/mixing → MixingRulesView → Yes → admin, platform_admin
├─ /admin/costs → CostsView → Yes → admin, platform_admin
├─ /admin/calendar → MarketingCalendarView → Yes → admin, platform_admin
├─ /admin/rules → BusinessRulesView → Yes → admin, platform_admin
├─ /admin/health → SchemaHealthView → Yes → admin, platform_admin

404 ROUTE
└─ /* → 404 Page → No → None
```

**Auth Pattern Summary:**
- `ProtectedRoute` with no props: requires any authenticated user
- `ProtectedRoute requireAdmin`: requires admin or platform_admin role
- `ProtectedRoute requireRole="spa_user"`: requires specific role
- `ProtectedRoute requireRole={['brand_admin', 'admin', 'platform_admin']}`: requires one of listed roles
- Routes without ProtectedRoute wrapper: publicly accessible

## SECTION 4: SUPABASE SCHEMA SUMMARY

Key database tables and their primary columns (from database.types.ts):

### Core Brand Content Tables

**canonical_protocols**
- id (uuid)
- protocol_name (string)
- category (string)
- target_concerns (string[])
- modalities_steps (json)
- typical_duration (string)
- allowed_products (string[])
- contraindications (string[])
- created_at (timestamp)

**pro_products** (Professional/Backbar)
- id (uuid)
- product_name (string)
- product_function (string)
- key_ingredients (string[])
- in_service_usage_allowed (string)
- contraindications (string[])
- created_at (timestamp)

**retail_products**
- id (uuid)
- product_name (string)
- product_function (string)
- target_concerns (string[])
- key_ingredients (string[])
- regimen_placement (string)
- created_at (timestamp)

**mixing_rules**
- id (uuid)
- rule_type (string)
- product_references (string[])
- rule_description (string)
- severity (string)
- created_at (timestamp)

**treatment_costs**
- id (uuid)
- item_type (string)
- item_reference (string)
- cost_per_unit (number)
- unit_type (string)
- typical_usage_amount (number)
- notes (string)
- created_at (timestamp)

### Spa Menu & Service Mapping Tables

**spa_menus**
- id (uuid)
- spa_name (string)
- upload_date (string)
- raw_menu_data (string)
- parse_status (string)
- created_at (timestamp)

**spa_services**
- id (uuid)
- menu_id (uuid, FK to spa_menus)
- service_name (string)
- category (string)
- duration (string)
- price (number)
- description (string)
- keywords (string[])
- created_at (timestamp)

**service_mappings**
- id (uuid)
- service_id (uuid, FK to spa_services)
- solution_type (string)
- solution_reference (string)
- match_type (string)
- confidence (string)
- rationale (string)
- custom_build_details (json)
- retail_attach (string[])
- cogs_status (string)
- cogs_amount (number)
- pricing_guidance (string)
- created_at (timestamp)

**Note:** The database.types.ts file is incomplete and only shows partial schema. 
The actual database has many additional tables including:
- user_profiles (auth/roles)
- brands (multi-tenant brand management)
- businesses (client management)
- plans (plan tracking)
- business_plan_outputs (plan results)
- canonical_protocol_steps (detailed protocol instructions)
- marketing_calendar (seasonal campaigns)
- brand_differentiation_points (sales talking points)
- retail_attach_recommendations (product pairing rules)
- service_gap_analysis (menu gap identification)
- opening_orders (inventory recommendations)
- phased_rollout_plans (implementation planning)
- implementation_readiness (training assessment)
- ai_concierge_chat_logs (AI interaction tracking)
- ai_concierge_starter_questions (contextual prompts)

## SECTION 5: KNOWN ISSUES

### 5.1 Console Statements (60+ instances)

**Error Handling (Legitimate Use)**
- src/main.tsx: Global error handlers (lines 2, 7, 21, 43)
- src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx: Error logging (line 29)
- src/lib/auth.tsx: Auth error logging (lines 54, 64, 131)

**Debug/Development Console.log**
- src/pages/business/PortalHome.tsx:46 - `console.log('BRANDS RETURNED:', data)`
  - Should be removed before production
- src/lib/aiConciergeEngine.ts:220 - `console.log('Concierge Retrieval Log:', retrievalLog)`
  - Consider moving to debug mode flag

**Legitimate Error Logging (40+ instances)**
- Most console.error calls are appropriate for error handling
- Found in async data fetching, Supabase query errors, and exception handling
- Examples: ProtocolsView, IngestionView, PlanResults, BrandDetail, etc.

**Legacy Warnings**
- src/lib/ingestionService.ts:32 - "ingestSpaMenu is a legacy stub function"
- src/lib/gapAnalysisEngine.ts:5 - "runGapAnalysis is a legacy stub function"
- src/lib/serviceMappingEngine.ts:5 - "runServiceMapping is a legacy stub function"
- These indicate incomplete migration from old architecture

### 5.2 TODO/FIXME Comments
**None Found** - No TODO or FIXME comments in source code

### 5.3 Placeholder Components & Routes

**Incomplete Features**
- /admin/submissions/:id → Placeholder div with "coming soon" message (App.tsx:154-162)
- SpaProductLibrary → Only 26 lines, likely placeholder
- SpaServiceLibrary → Only 26 lines, likely placeholder
- SpaConcierge → Only 18 lines, incomplete

### 5.4 Legacy Code & Migration Issues

**Dual Portal Architecture**
- Both /spa/* and /portal/* routes exist
- /spa/signup redirects to /portal/signup (App.tsx:120)
- Suggests ongoing migration from spa-specific to business-generic portal
- spa_user role still exists but business_user role is preferred

**Stub Functions**
- ingestSpaMenu (ingestionService.ts) - marked as legacy
- runGapAnalysis (gapAnalysisEngine.ts) - marked as legacy
- runServiceMapping (serviceMappingEngine.ts) - marked as legacy
- These functions exist but log warnings and may not be fully functional

### 5.5 Potential RLS/Security Issues

**Brand Discovery RLS Concerns**
- Recent migrations (20260216*) suggest RLS issues were being fixed
- Files: fix_brands_public_visibility.sql, fix_brands_rls_use_status_column.sql
- Anonymous access to brand content added in 20260216204527
- Indicates recent security model changes that may need testing

**Multi-Tenant Scoping**
- 20260124004432_update_rls_for_multi_tenant_brand_scoping_v2.sql
- Complex RLS rules for brand_id scoping across many tables
- High risk area for data leakage if policies are incorrect

### 5.6 Missing Error Handling

**Unguarded Async Operations**
- Many useEffect hooks with async functions lack comprehensive error boundaries
- Examples in: Plans.tsx, Dashboard.tsx, PlanDetail.tsx
- Errors are logged but may not display user-friendly messages

**No Retry Logic**
- Database queries fail immediately without retry
- Could benefit from exponential backoff on transient failures
- Exception: auth.tsx has retry logic in fetchProfile (3 retries)

### 5.7 Type Safety Issues

**database.types.ts Incomplete**
- Only defines ~10 tables but migrations show 40+ tables exist
- Missing types for: user_profiles, brands, businesses, plans, etc.
- May lead to runtime errors with untyped database queries

**Loose JSON Types**
- Many columns use Json type without specific interfaces
- Examples: modalities_steps, custom_build_details
- Could benefit from discriminated unions or Zod schemas

### 5.8 Performance Concerns

**Large Data Fetches**
- BrandDetail.tsx loads 4+ tables in parallel without pagination
- ProtocolsView, ProProductsView load entire tables
- Could cause slowness with large datasets

**No Data Caching**
- Every route navigation refetches data
- React Query or similar would improve UX
- Particularly noticeable in admin portal navigation

### 5.9 Unused Imports/Components

**Components Without Clear Usage**
- DevOnlyMasterLinks (64 lines) - Purpose unclear, may be debug only
- SpaOnboardingWizard (512 lines) - Not referenced in any routes
- Phase2IngestionPanel (305 lines) - Not in main routes
- ImplementationPlannerView (542 lines) - Not clearly routed
- AIConciergeLogsView (370 lines) - Admin tool, but route unclear

### 5.10 Data Integrity & Validation

**No Client-Side Validation**
- Forms in Login, Signup, PlanWizard lack validation
- Relies entirely on database constraints
- Should add Zod or similar for better UX

**Missing NULL Checks**
- Many optional fields accessed without guards
- Examples: profile?.spa_name, plan?.avg_risk_score
- TypeScript non-null assertions may hide bugs

---
